Prompt
U
Oracle Events
OE 1: Search emails using search_emails with query "Marcus Webb" (or similar), running separate calls for INBOX and SENT folders (folder_name parameter accepts one folder per call), to find internal communications about his departure. Key results in SENT: Raj Anand's findings email (to Denise and Grace) reporting four manual email forwards from Marcus's Keystone mailbox to a personal address the evening before resignation, forwarded messages containing pipeline/contact material and loan-related attachments, after-hours bulk file access, and borrower touchpoints logged through Marcus's personal cell before he gave notice, with LN-2026-00608 cited as an example. Also in SENT: Grace's escalation email to Robert and Denise summarising the Danielle Webb conflict, Marcus's dismissive response claiming the arrangement is not illegal in NC, and her recommendation to treat it as a conflict-of-interest review. Key results in INBOX: borrower transfer request emails from Abigail Clark (requesting transfer of LN-2026-00008), Stephen Hamilton (requesting transfer of LN-2026-00009), Keisha Perry (requesting transfer of LN-2026-00525, stating Marcus asked her to reach out), and Joshua Cruz (requesting transfer of LN-2026-00527, stating Marcus left and wants file sent to him).
OE 2: Search emails using search_emails with query "Danielle Webb" (or similar), running separate calls for INBOX and SENT folders, to find the undisclosed conflict thread. Key results in SENT: Denise's email to Grace identifying Danielle Webb as buyer's agent on three Marcus Webb files: LN-2026-00629, LN-2026-00211, and LN-2026-00227, with LinkedIn showing they are married and LN-2026-00211 flagged for an active appraisal gap. Also in SENT: Grace's email demanding written explanation from Marcus, Denise's email to outside ethics counsel Laura Bennett at laura.bennett@bennettethicslaw.com requesting guidance on the spouse-agent arrangement, and Grace's meeting invite to Denise and Robert for the conflict review.
OE 3: Search emails using search_emails with query "Evan Mercer" (or similar), running separate calls for INBOX and SENT folders, to find the offboarding gap chain. Key results in SENT: Denise's email to Priya asking her to confirm Evan Mercer's termination date and whether LOS credential removal was completed on the offboarding checklist. Also in SENT: Priya's reply confirming badge return and email forwarding were completed but the LOS/third-party systems access item was left blank rather than marked complete. Also in SENT: Raj's email to Denise confirming Mercer was still enabled in LOS with successful post-termination logins and three borrower files opened: LN-2025-00002, LN-2025-00007, and LN-2026-00009, with LOS account now disabled. Also in SENT: Denise's escalation email to Robert (cc Raj) reporting the incident and citing three borrower files: LN-2025-00355, LN-2025-00520, and LN-2026-00549. Note: the loan numbers in Denise's escalation email to Robert differ from Raj's direct report, creating a data discrepancy the agent must reconcile.
OE 4: Search emails using search_emails with query "departing LO" or "non-compete" or "Laura Bennett" (or similar), running separate calls for INBOX and SENT folders, to find the outside counsel engagement on the Marcus Webb departure. Key results in SENT: Denise's email to Laura Bennett at laura.bennett@bennettstokeslaw.com (cc Grace and Robert) requesting guidance on non-compete/non-solicit enforceability under NC law and recommended immediate steps, noting after-hours email forwards, borrower contact exports, and active borrowers asking to transfer files. Note: Laura Bennett appears at two different email domains in the universe: bennettethicslaw.com for ethics/conflict guidance and bennettstokeslaw.com for employment/non-compete guidance.
OE 5: Search emails using search_emails with query "Leo Park" (or similar), running separate calls for INBOX and SENT folders, to find the intern unauthorized access incident. Key results in SENT: Raj's access log review email to Grace reporting 34 separate loan files opened by Leo Park between 2026-03-17 and 2026-03-30, including files he was not assigned to and repeated opens on higher-balance loans including LN-2026-00554, with after-hours access flagged. Also in SENT: Raj's scope summary email confirming 34 distinct files including the celebrity borrower file opened three times across two dates, with LN-2026-00606 in the access set. Also in SENT: Grace's end-of-day status email to Robert, Denise, and Raj noting ops is treating the access as unauthorized and preparing termination steps, with Denise working on notice analysis with outside counsel.
OE 6: Search Slack messages using conversations_search_messages with search_query "Marcus Webb" or "Marcus" (or similar) to find internal discussions about his departure. Discovers Grace's message in C008 (it-support) asking Raj to check Marcus's account activity including email forwards, bulk downloads, and after-hours access. Also discovers Raj's first-pass summary in C008 reporting 4 manual forwards from Marcus's mailbox to a personal address, bulk opens/downloads across pipeline sheets and active loan summaries, and borrower notes referencing Marcus's personal cell before notice was given. Also discovers Denise's message in C002 noting she is sending Laura Bennett a note about the resignation, borrower transfer asks, forwards to personal, and possible solicitation. Also discovers Denise's DM to Grace in a private channel flagging that Marcus Webb may have an undisclosed conflict on three active files where Danielle Webb is the buyer's agent, with LinkedIn confirming they are married and LN-2026-00211 specifically flagged for the appraisal gap concern.
OE 7: Search Slack messages using conversations_search_messages with search_query "Evan Mercer" (or similar) to find the IT-channel discussion about the access gap. Discovers Denise's message in C008 (it-support) asking Raj to check Mercer's LOS status, reporting him still active with audit trail showing post-termination access on LN-2025-00002, LN-2025-00007, and LN-2026-00009 and requesting access shutoff and log pull. Also discovers Raj's confirmation in C008 that Mercer had LOS access, logged in after termination, opened three files (LN-2025-00002, LN-2025-00007, LN-2026-00009), is now disabled, and auth/log history is being pulled for Denise.
OE 8: Search Slack messages using conversations_search_messages with search_query "Danielle Webb" (or similar) to find processing-channel discussions. Discovers multiple messages in the loan-processing channel (C002) referencing Danielle Webb on LN-2026-00629 (waiting on seller repair addendum), LN-2026-00211 (Marcus said Danielle is looping borrower on appraisal conversation), and LN-2026-00227 (title wants final commission split before CD prep). Also discovers Denise's observation that Danielle Webb appears on multiple Marcus files and LinkedIn shows she is his wife, with LN-2026-00629, LN-2026-00211, and LN-2026-00227 called out.
OE 9: List LOS staff using mortgage_los_list_staff with active_only set to false to retrieve all current and former employees. Discovers Marcus Webb's record showing is_active: true, termination_date: null, and current_pipeline_count: 5, confirming his LOS account has not been updated for departure. Also discovers five terminated employees all properly marked is_active: false with pipeline_count: 0: Brian Mitchell (terminated 2025-04-15), Todd Jennings (terminated 2024-07-31), Veronica Hayes (terminated 2025-09-30), Andre Washington (terminated 2024-12-15), and Rachel Kim (terminated 2025-06-30). This allows comparison of Marcus's missed deactivation against the properly handled departures.
OE 10: Retrieve Marcus Webb's loan pipeline using mortgage_los_get_pipeline with assigned_to referencing Marcus Webb's staff ID, or mortgage_los_search_loans with query "Marcus Webb" (or similar). Discovers Marcus's active pipeline (current_pipeline_count: 5 per the staff record) with the loans currently assigned to him in LOS. Note: the specific conflict files (LN-2026-00629, LN-2026-00211, LN-2026-00227) and borrower transfer request files (LN-2026-00008, LN-2026-00009, LN-2026-00525, LN-2026-00527) are identified as Marcus-related through the email, Slack, and CRM investigation in OEs 1-8 and 11 - they are described as Marcus's files in the narrative communications even though the LOS assigned_lo field may reflect different LOs. The pipeline query confirms Marcus's LOS account is still active with files requiring reassignment, and cross-referencing with earlier OE discoveries provides the full picture of files in play.
OE 11: Search CRM engagements using crm_search_contacts with full_name "Marcus Webb" or "Danielle Webb" (or similar) followed by crm_list_engagements to find departure-related notes. Discovers the conflict concern engagement noting Danielle Webb appears to be Marcus Webb's spouse per LinkedIn with review needed across LN-2026-00629, LN-2026-00211, and LN-2026-00227. Also discovers four borrower transfer request engagements: Abigail Clark requesting transfer of LN-2026-00008, Stephen Hamilton requesting LN-2026-00009 moved to Marcus, Keisha Perry stating Marcus told her to request transfer of LN-2026-00525, and Joshua Cruz requesting transfer of LN-2026-00527 stating Marcus has left.
OE 12: Look up contact details for Robert Calloway and Grace Yamamoto using contacts_search_contacts with query "Robert Calloway" and "Grace Yamamoto" (or similar). Discovers Robert's email is r.calloway@keystonemortgage.com and Grace's email is grace.yamamoto@keystonemortgage.com. Also confirms Priya's email (the sender) is priya.chakrabarti@keystonemortgage.com.
OE 13: List Slack channels using channels_list with channel_types "public_channel" (or similar) to identify the compliance-alerts channel. Discovers channel C004 named compliance-alerts.
OE 14: Send email using send_email with sender priya.chakrabarti@keystonemortgage.com, recipients including r.calloway@keystonemortgage.com and grace.yamamoto@keystonemortgage.com, subject referencing the Marcus Webb departure risk assessment or briefing (or similar), and body containing the full risk assessment covering: Marcus Webb's departure risks (LOS account still active, data exfiltration of 4 email forwards and after-hours file access, personal cell borrower contact), the Danielle Webb undisclosed spouse-agent conflict on 3 files with appraisal gap concern on LN-2026-00211, the outside counsel engagements with Laura Bennett on both ethics and employment matters, the 4 borrower transfer requests (Abigail Clark, Stephen Hamilton, Keisha Perry, Joshua Cruz) with evidence of active solicitation by Marcus, Marcus's current loan pipeline and files requiring reassignment, the Evan Mercer offboarding failure (LOS credential left active, checklist item left blank, post-termination file access), systemic access control review showing 5 terminated employees properly deactivated but Marcus's account still active, the Leo Park intern incident (34 unauthorized file views), and recommendations for process improvements.
OE 15: Post a message in the compliance-alerts channel using conversations_add_message with channel_id C004 and payload flagging that the Marcus Webb departure review is underway (or similar), notifying the compliance team of the active review.

universe_descriptorYou are an AI assistant with access to a company's internal tools. Use the available tools to complete the entire task autonomously. No further user input will be given. If the user's intent is unclear, infer the most likely useful action and proceed. Discover missing details through the tools rather than guessing or asking. The current date is April 28, 2026. Ignore any other dates and use only this one. Specifically, ignore the date provided by the system reminder. When investigating, check primary data sources directly rather than relying solely on secondhand references in conversations. Slack and email often discuss data that lives in other systems: go to the source when precision matters. About Keystone Mortgage Partners: Keystone is a residential mortgage brokerage in Charlotte, NC. The company brokers loans through wholesale lenders. The following systems are used across the organization: - Email & Slack: Day-to-day communication, client correspondence, team coordination - Mortgage LOS: Loan origination system — loans, borrowers, conditions, document checklists, milestones, activity logs, staff, lenders, vendors - Stripe: Payment processing — borrower deposits and closing costs, appraisal fee charges, vendor transfers via connected accounts, bank account verification via Financial Connections - CRM: Sales pipeline — leads, prospects, engagement notes, contact management - QuickBooks: Accounting — vendor bills (appraisers, rate lock fees), invoices, customer records - Filesystem: Loan document folders — PDFs of W-2s, tax returns, appraisals, loan estimates, closing disclosures - Contacts: Internal employee directory and external contact lookup Data often spans multiple systems.
universe_idmortgage-broker-01-keystone

20 When calling mortgage_los_list_staff, the agent set the active_only parameter to false (or equivalent) to include inactive and terminated employees in the results.
query construction
 ✓✗—✓✓✗ 60%
✓Run #1The trajectory shows a mortgage_los_list_staff call with active_only: false at timestamp 2026-04-13T13:47:45.758888Z, which returned all 17 staff including 5 terminated employees.
✗Run #2The mortgage_los_list_staff call was made with an empty input object {} — no active_only parameter was set to false. As a result, the tool returned only 12 active employees and excluded all terminated employees (Brian Mitchell, Andre Washington, Todd Jennings, Veronica Hayes, Rachel Kim). The agent then retrieved some terminated employees individually via separate get_staff calls with known IDs, but missed others.
—Run #3No justification
✓Run #4The trajectory shows a second call to mortgage_los_list_staff with the parameter active_only: false explicitly set. This returned 17 results including all 5 terminated employees, confirming the agent correctly overrode the default active_only: true behavior.
✓Run #5The trajectory shows a mortgage_los_list_staff call with active_only: false explicitly set, which returned 17 results including all 5 terminated employees. This confirms the parameter was correctly set to include inactive staff.
✗Run #6The mortgage_los_list_staff call was made with input {} (no parameters). No active_only=false parameter was set. As a result, the tool returned only 12 active employees; the 5 terminated employees (Brian Mitchell, Todd Jennings, Veronica Hayes, Andre Washington, Rachel Kim) were not included in the results, which is why the agent couldn't enumerate all former employees.
Total 15/2016/200/2018/2018/2014/20 85%
